description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a surface pattern resembling white or light gray bricks, separated by dark mortar lines. The block has a uniform, repeating brick texture on all visible sides, giving it the appearance of a section of a brick wall. The bricks are slightly rough and uneven, with subtle surface imperfections and a matte finish. The mortar lines are consistent in width and color, providing a clear contrast to the lighter bricks. The block appears solid and heavy, with no visible openings, engravings, or decorative features. Its overall look suggests it is designed for use as a barrier or structural element in an urban environment.
description: A solid, rectangular stone block with a white brick pattern and dark mortar lines, featuring a rough, matte surface.
description_view_0: Front view shows a flat face with a regular white brick pattern and dark mortar lines.
description_view_1: Side view displays the same brick pattern, with slight variations in the brick alignment and surface texture.
description_view_2: Opposite face to the front, also showing the brick pattern and consistent mortar lines.
description_view_3: Back view mirrors the front, with the same brick arrangement and surface details.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or decorative block
attribute_installation_method: set in mortar or concrete foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with minor textural imperfections.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall or barrier
